description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: The show bgp ipv4 unicast command output can show an EVPN route while the output of the show ip route command indicates that it has anECMP route. Senario: When the same prefix is learned from two address families on on BGP (for example from BGP IPv4 unicast with a lesser AS-PATH length and from L2VPN EVPN address-family with a higher AS-PATH length) BGP RIB would pick the EVPN route as the best.

description: Symptom: VSX sync is no longer syncing after a configuring BGP to redistribute to OSPF with a route map. Scenario: This issue can occur on a pair of switches in a VSX configuration that are syncing for BGP when a user configures redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map on the primary switch. VSX sync will not sync the redistribution configuration or any other new configuration. Workaround: Disable BGP syncing or remove the redistribution for BGP to OSPF with a route map.

description: Symptom: The show bgp ipv4 unicast command output can show an EVPN route while the output of the show ip route command indicates that it has anECMP route. Senario: When the same prefix is learned from two address families on on BGP (for example from BGP IPv4 unicast with a lesser AS-PATH length and from L2VPN EVPN address-family with a higher AS-PATH length) BGP RIB would pick the EVPN route as the best.

description: Symptom: Some hosts may experience connectivity issues. Scenario: In an EVPN environment with ARP suppression enabled, random hosts could experience connectivity issues due to missing the associated ARP entry. Workaround: Temporarily add andremove a static ARP entry using the following commands Copy interface vlan <vlan_id>
